differential_diagnoses: Tumefactive demyelinating lesion (e.g., MS or ADEM)
Infectious etiology (e.g., fungal or parasitic granuloma)
next_steps: Perform lumbar puncture for CSF analysis (cell count, protein, glucose, cytology, flow cytometry, oligoclonal bands, EBV PCR)
Obtain permanent histological staining and immunohistochemistry (e.g., CD20, CD3, GFAP, KI-67, EBER) of biopsy tissue
Review biopsy tissue for specific viral or fungal markers if lymphoma markers are negative
Continue high-dose corticosteroids to reduce mass effect and reassess neurological status
Perform systemic evaluation if lymphoma diagnosis is confirmed (e.g., PET-CT, slit-lamp exam)
